remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Dry AMD: slow progressive loss; wet AMD progresses rapidly without treatment (50% of untreated reach legal blindness within 2 years); no spontaneous remission of either form
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Anti-VEGF (ranibizumab, aflibercept, bevacizumab): 30-40% vision gain in wet AMD (MARINA, ANCHOR trials); maintain vision in 90%+ vs 62% in placebo; high-dose vitamin supplementation (AREDS2): 25% reduction in progression from intermediate to advanced dry AMD; port delivery system (Susvimo): 6-monthly refillable implant equivalent to monthly injections
remission.gap_size: Large — treatment burden (monthly injections) is the primary barrier to adequate treatment in wet AMD; dry AMD has no proven disease-modifying treatment until 2023
remission.barrier_detail: Monthly anti-VEGF injection burden leads to under-treatment in real-world settings; 50% of wet AMD patients in routine care lose vision compared to 5-10% in trials; geographic atrophy (dry AMD) had no approved treatment until pegcetacoplan (Syfovre, 2023)
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — pegcetacoplan (Syfovre, complement C3 inhibitor) and avacincaptad pegol (Izervay, complement C5 inhibitor) approved for geographic atrophy — first ever treatments for dry AMD; ranibizumab port delivery system (Susvimo) approved 2021; anti-VEGF since 2006
remission.outdated_treatments: Photocoagulation laser for wet AMD (replaced by anti-VEGF since 2006; still used in some settings for extrafoveal disease); photodynamic therapy (PDT) with verteporfin (replaced by anti-VEGF except selected cases); vitamin E supplementation alone without the full AREDS2 formula
remission.adjunctive_emerging: High-dose AREDS2 formula supplements (standard of care for intermediate AMD); lutein/zeaxanthin from diet or supplementation; Mediterranean diet pattern (observational data for AMD prevention); blue-light filtering lenses (theoretical, limited clinical evidence); omega-3 fatty acids (did not add benefit in AREDS2 but observational evidence supports)
remission.frontier_signal: Subretinal gene therapy (RGX-314, ADVM-022 — delivering anti-VEGF gene to retinal cells, avoiding injections); AAV-CNGB3 for achromatopsia; faricimab (dual Ang2+VEGF blockade — extended dosing up to 16 weeks, TENAYA/LUCERNE); OPT-302 (VEGF-C/D trap) combination; complement pathway targeting for GA progression
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
